title: Image processing systems and methods
abstract: We describe a method of capturing writing or drawing on a whiteboard. The method comprises: inputting camera data for a succession of image frames, wherein the camera data is from a camera directed towards the whiteboard and the image frames comprise successive images of the whiteboard from the camera; and user filter processing data from said image frames to remove parts of said image frames corresponding to parts of a user or user pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard. The user-filter processing comprises filtering to distinguish between motion of the user/user pen parts in the image frames and writing/drawing image information in the image frames which appears or changes during said writing or drawing but which is thereafter substantially unchanging. The method outputs writing/drawing data from the user filter-processing, this defining captured writing or drawing from the whiteboard.
claims: Claims CLAIMS: 1. A method of capturing writing or drawing on a whiteboard, the method comprising: inputting camera data for a succession of image frames, wherein said camera data is from a camera directed towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said camera; user filter processing data from said image frames to remove parts of said image frames corresponding to parts of a user or user pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard; wherein said user filter processing comprises filtering to distinguish between motion of said user/user pen parts in said image frames and writing/drawing image information in said image frames which appears or changes during said writing or drawing but which is thereafter substantially unchanging; and outputting writing/drawing data from said user filter processing, wherein said writing/drawing data defines captured writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 2. A method as claimed in claim 1 wherein said user filter processing comprises subdividing said image frames into blocks for blockwise processing, said blockwise processing comprising determining when the image content of a block has changed by less than a threshold over a user filter time interval; and in response identifying that the block does not include user/pen parts. 3. A method as claimed in claim 2 wherein said determining comprises comparing corresponding blocks in frames at different times to determine a measure of a difference between pixel values of the blocks; comparing said measure against said threshold; determining a time period for which said measure is less than said threshold; and identifying that the block does not include user/pen parts when said measure is less than said threshold for greater than said user filter time interval. 4. A method as claimed in claim 1 , 2 or 3 wherein said user filter processing comprises subdividing said image frames into blocks for blockwise processing, wherein the area of a block in square pixels is at least ten times the area of the tip of a pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 5. A method as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising brightness or colour normalisation processing of said image frames for said user filter processing. 6. A method as claim 5 further comprising subdividing said image frames into blocks; wherein said brightness or colour normalisation processing comprises normalising the brightnesses or colours of pixels within said blocks. 7. A method as claimed in claim 6 wherein said normalising the brightnesses or colours of pixels within said blocks comprises normalising according to a brightness or colour percentile greater than the 50 th percentile. 8. A method as claimed in claim 1 wherein said user filter processing comprises temporally filtering said image frames with a user filter time constant to distinguish said user/pen from said writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 9. A method as claimed in claim 8 further comprising spatially filtering said image frames to attenuate changes in illumination across said whiteboard as seen by said camera. 10. A method as claimed in any preceding claim wherein said camera data is from a camera directed at an acute angle towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said acute angle; the method further comprising distortion correction processing to correct for distortion resulting from said acute angle direction of said camera. 1 1. A method as claimed in claim 10 wherein said distortion correction processing comprises applying a projective transform to said camera data to provide corrected image frame data for said image frames, and wherein said user filter processing operates on said corrected image frame data. 12. A method as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising applying a fisheye distortion correction to said camera data. 13. A method, in particular as claimed in any preceding claim, the method comprising reprocessing data from said user filter processing to provide said writing/drawing data, said reprocessing comprising identifying writing/drawing pixels and remapping one or more colours in said camera data into one or more replacement colours in said output writing/drawing data. 14. A method as claimed in any preceding claim further comprising cleaning said data from said user filter processing by blockwise thresholding. 15. A method as claimed in any preceding claim comprising successively updating said writing/drawing data to provide dynamic capture of said writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 16. A method, in particular as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein said output writing/drawing data comprises vector graphics data, in particular time-indexed vector graphics data. 17. A method, in particular as claimed in any preceding claim, the method further comprising controlling operation of the method by interpreting one or more commands captured within said image frames of said camera data. 18. A method, in particular as claimed in any preceding claim, the method further comprising inputting audio data and outputting synchronisation data in combination with said writing/drawing data to enable replay of the audio data associated with an identified part of said writing or drawing. 19. A method, in particular as claimed in any preceding claim, the method further comprising adapting said output writing/drawing data to simulate the output from a video camera. 20. A method of dynamically sharing a whiteboard, the method comprising using a camera to capture a succession of images of the whiteboard; processing the images using the method of any one of claims 1 to 19; and transmitting said writing/drawing data to a remote location for sharing. 21. A non-transitory data carrier carrying processor control code to, when running, implement the method of any preceding claim. 22. Whiteboard sharing apparatus comprising: a camera to be directed to capture an image from a whiteboard; and signal processing apparatus, coupled to said camera and configured to: input camera data for a succession of image frames, wherein said camera data is from a camera directed towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said camera user filter process data from said image frames to remove parts of said image frames corresponding to parts of a user or user pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard; wherein said user filter processing comprises filtering to distinguish between motion of said user/user pen parts in said image frames and writing/drawing image information in said image frames which appears or changes during said writing or drawing but which is thereafter substantially unchanging; and output writing/drawing data from said user filter processing, wherein said writing/drawing data defines captured writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 23. Whiteboard sharing apparatus as claimed in claim 22 wherein said camera is directed at an acute angle towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said acute angle; and wherein said signal processing apparatus is further configured to process said image frames to correct for distortion resulting from said acute angle direction of said camera. 24. Whiteboard sharing apparatus as claimed in claim 22 or 23 wherein said user filter processing comprises subdividing said image frames into blocks for blockwise processing, said blockwise processing comprising determining when the image content of a block has changed by less than a threshold over a user filter time interval; and in response identifying that the block does not include user/pen parts. 25. Whiteboard sharing apparatus as claimed in claim 22, 23 or 24 wherein said signal processing apparatus is further configured to subdividing said image frames into blocks and normalise the brightnesses or colours of pixels within said blocks prior to said user filter processing. 26. A whiteboard image capture system, the system comprising: a camera configured to capture an image of the whiteboard viewed from an acute angle; and a linear polariser having a plane substantially perpendicular to a plane of said whiteboard and having an axis of polarisation substantially perpendicular to the plane of the whiteboard or within 30Â° of the perpendicular to the plane of the whiteboard; wherein said camera is configured to capture said image of the whiteboard through said polariser; and wherein a normal to said polariser is directed in a different direction to a direction defined by an optic axis of said camera. 27. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 26 wherein said camera has a rectilinear lens. 28. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 27 wherein an optical axis of said camera is directed towards said whiteboard at an angle of less than 15Â° to a normal to a surface of the whiteboard. 29. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 28 wherein said optic axis of said camera is directed towards said whiteboard at an angle of between 12Â° and 0.5Â° to a normal to a surface of the whiteboard. 30. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in any one of claims 26 to 29 wherein said polariser is curved about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the whiteboard. 31. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 30 wherein said polariser is curved to define part of a curved surface of a cylinder having an axis normal to the plane of the whiteboard. 32. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 30 wherein said polariser is located at a greater distance from said camera than a location defined by an intersection of the optic axis of the camera with a plane defined by a surface of the whiteboard. 33. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in any one of claims 26 to 32 wherein said camera is located laterally adjacent an upper or lower edge of said whiteboard and is displaced vertically away from the plane of the whiteboard. 34. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 33 wherein said optical axis of said camera is directed to miss or just intersect said edge of said whiteboard. 35. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 33 or 34 wherein said camera is mounted approximately at a mid-point of said upper or lower edge. 36. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in any one of claims 26 to 35 wherein said acute angle is less than 45Â° or less than 30Â°. 37. A method of capturing writing or drawing on a whiteboard using the whiteboard image capture system of any one of claim 26 to 36, the method comprising: inputting camera data for a succession of image frames, wherein said camera data is from a camera directed towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said camera; user filter processing data from said image frames to remove parts of said image frames corresponding to parts of a user or user pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard; wherein said user filter processing comprises filtering to distinguish between motion of said user/user pen parts in said image frames and writing/drawing image information in said image frames which appears or changes during said writing or drawing but which is thereafter substantially unchanging; and outputting writing/drawing data from said user filter processing, wherein said writing/drawing data defines captured writing or drawing on said whiteboard. 38. A method as claimed in claim 37 wherein said user filter processing comprises subdividing said image frames into blocks for blockwise processing, said blockwise processing comprising determining when the image content of a block has changed by less than a threshold over a user filter time interval; and in response identifying that the block does not include user/pen parts. 39. A method as claimed in claim 38 wherein said determining comprises comparing corresponding blocks in frames at different times to determine a measure of a difference between pixel values of the blocks; comparing said measure against said threshold; determining a time period for which said measure is less than said threshold; and identifying that the block does not include user/pen parts when said measure is less than said threshold for greater than said user filter time interval. 40. An image capture method, the method comprising: capturing an image of a surface using a camera viewing the surface at an acute angle to the surface and having an optical axis directed towards said surface at an angle of less than 15Â° to a normal to the surface; arranging a linear polariser at a location laterally between an intersection of the optic axis of the camera with a plane defined by said surface and a portion of said surface viewed by the camera; arranging said linear polariser to be generally perpendicular to a plane of said surface; aligning an axis of polarisation of said polariser generally perpendicular to the plane of the surface; and capturing said image using a said camera viewing through said linear polariser. 41. Whiteboard sharing apparatus comprising: a camera to be directed to capture an image from a whiteboard; and signal processing apparatus, coupled to said camera and configured to: input camera data for a succession of image frames, wherein said camera data is from a camera directed towards said whiteboard and said image frames comprise successive images of said whiteboard from said camera filter data from said image frames to remove parts of said image frames corresponding to parts of a user or user pen writing or drawing on said whiteboard; and output writing/drawing data from said user filter processing, wherein said writing/drawing data defines captured writing or drawing on said whiteboard; and a polariser having a plane substantially perpendicular to a plane of said whiteboard. 42. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 41 wherein said polariser is curved about an axis perpendicular to the plane of the whiteboard. 43. A whiteboard image capture system, the system comprising: a camera with a rectilinear lens configured to capture an image of the whiteboard viewed from an acute angle; and wherein said camera is located laterally adjacent an upper or lower edge of said whiteboard and is displaced vertically away from the plane of the whiteboard; and wherein an optic axis of said camera is directed towards said whiteboard at an angle of less than 15Â° to a normal to a surface of the whiteboard. 44. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 43 wherein said optic axis of said camera is directed towards said whiteboard at an angle of between 12Â° and 0.5Â° to a normal to a surface of the whiteboard. 45. A whiteboard image capture system as claimed in claim 43 or 44 further comprising a linear polariser at a location laterally between an intersection of an optic axis of the camera with a plane defined by the whiteboard and a portion of said whiteboard viewed by the camera, wherein said linear polariser is generally perpendicular to a plane of said surface and has an axis of polarisation generally perpendicular to the plane of the whiteboard.
